test_cases.title: Successful password reset link request with valid registered email
preconditions: A user account exists with a known registered email address
The email delivery service is operational
steps: Navigate to the forgot password page
Enter the registered email address in the email field
Submit the password reset request
Check the inbox of the registered email address
test_cases.expected_result: A password reset email containing a unique reset link is delivered to the specified registered email address within a reasonable time.

test_cases.title: Password reset link expires after 60 minutes
preconditions: A valid password reset link has been generated and sent to the user's email
The system clock can be manipulated or waited on in the test environment
steps: Request a password reset for a registered account
Retrieve the password reset link from the email
Wait 61 minutes without using the link
Open the password reset link in a browser
Attempt to submit a new password using the expired link
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request and displays an error indicating the reset link has expired; no password change is applied.

test_cases.title: Password reset link cannot be reused after successful reset
preconditions: A valid password reset link has been generated and sent to the user's email
The link has not yet been used and has not expired
steps: Open the password reset link from the email
Enter and submit a new valid password
Confirm the password reset is successful
Attempt to open the same password reset link again in a browser
Attempt to submit a new password using the same link
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the second use of the link and displays an error indicating the link has already been used; no further password change is applied.

test_cases.title: Old password is invalidated immediately after successful reset
preconditions: A user account exists with a known current password
A password reset has been completed successfully using a valid reset link
steps: Complete a successful password reset, setting a new password
Navigate to the login page
Enter the user's email and the old (pre-reset) password
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies login and displays an authentication error; the old password is no longer accepted.

test_cases.title: Password reset request submitted for a non-registered email address
preconditions: The email address to be used does not correspond to any existing account
steps: Navigate to the forgot password page
Enter an email address that is not registered in the system
Submit the password reset request
Check the inbox of the non-registered email address
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not send a reset email to the unregistered address; the response to the user does not reveal whether the email address exists in the system (to prevent user enumeration).

test_cases.title: Password reset link used within the 60-minute validity window is accepted
preconditions: A valid password reset link has been generated and sent to the user's email
Less than 60 minutes have elapsed since the link was generated
steps: Request a password reset for a registered account
Retrieve the password reset link from the email
Open the link within 59 minutes of its generation
Enter and submit a new valid password
test_cases.expected_result: The system accepts the new password and confirms the password reset was successful.

assumptions: The system provides a dedicated 'forgot password' page or entry point accessible without authentication.
The system is expected not to reveal whether an email address is registered (anti-enumeration behavior), as this is a standard security practice implied by the email-based reset flow.
A 'new valid password' meets whatever password policy the system enforces, even though no policy is specified in this requirement.
The reset link is unique per request; requesting a new reset link invalidates any previously issued unexpired links for the same account.
The 60-minute expiry is measured from the time the link is generated, not from the time the email is delivered.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the system's response when a reset is requested for an unregistered email — it is ambiguous whether an error should be shown or a neutral message displayed; (2) whether multiple concurrent reset links for the same account are allowed and how they interact; (3) password complexity or validation rules for the new password; (4) whether the user should be automatically logged in after a successful reset; (5) rate limiting or lockout behavior for repeated reset requests, which is a security concern not addressed. Coverage for rate limiting and concurrent link invalidation is absent due to these gaps.
